name: test-automator
description: Master AI-powered test automation with modern frameworks,
capabilities: Design comprehensive test automation strategies for microservices and distributed systems
Implement AI-powered self-healing tests with tools like Testsigma, Testim, and Applitools
Guide TDD workflows with red-green-refactor cycle automation and metrics tracking
Integrate testing pipelines with Jenkins, GitLab CI, and GitHub Actions
Generate test cases using natural language processing and machine learning optimization
Provide cross-browser, mobile, and API testing framework recommendations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill containing no executable code, scripts, or external command invocations. Static analysis scanned 0 files and found 0 security issues. The skill provides test automation guidance through natural language instructions only. No network access, filesystem operations, or code execution patterns detected. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Manual testing and fragile test suites slow down development and reduce confidence. This skill delivers expert guidance on modern test automation, self-healing tests, and AI-driven quality engineering to build robust testing ecosystems.
limitations: Cannot execute tests directly - provides guidance and code examples only
Requires access to your codebase and testing infrastructure for specific implementations
Framework recommendations depend on your technology stack and team expertise
AI-powered testing tools require separate subscriptions and setup

useCases.title: QA Engineer Building Test Strategy
useCases.description: Design a comprehensive test automation strategy for a microservices architecture with appropriate framework selection, CI/CD integration, and quality metrics.

useCases.title: Developer Practicing TDD
useCases.description: Get real-time guidance on test-driven development workflows, including failing test generation, minimal implementation patterns, and refactoring with regression safety.

useCases.title: DevOps Team Optimizing CI/CD
useCases.description: Implement parallel test execution, dynamic test selection, and containerized testing environments with automated reporting and threshold validation.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Test Framework Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to set up test automation for a React application. Recommend a testing framework combination for unit, integration, and end-to-end tests, and provide a starter configuration.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new project with no existing tests

promptTemplates.title: TDD Workflow Guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement a user authentication feature using TDD. Guide me through writing the failing test first, then minimal implementation, and finally refactoring with safety validation.
promptTemplates.scenario: Following test-driven development for a new feature

promptTemplates.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Our tests run sequentially and take 45 minutes. Analyze our GitHub Actions workflow and suggest optimizations for parallel execution, test selection, and caching strategies.

promptTemplates.title: AI-Powered Self-Healing Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Our UI tests break frequently due to selector changes. Evaluate AI-powered self-healing testing tools and provide an implementation plan for migrating our existing Selenium tests.

bestPractices: Follow the test pyramid: prioritize fast unit tests over slow end-to-end tests for efficient feedback loops
Design tests as living documentation with descriptive names and clear assertions that explain intent
Implement test data management with factories and fixtures to ensure isolation and reproducibility
antiPatterns: Testing implementation details instead of observable behavior, causing brittle tests that break during refactoring
Creating oversized end-to-end tests that cover multiple concerns and are difficult to debug when they fail
Ignoring test execution time and running all tests sequentially instead of optimizing for parallel execution

faq.question: What testing frameworks do you recommend for my stack?
faq.answer: Framework selection depends on your technology stack. For JavaScript: Jest and Playwright. For Python: pytest and Playwright. For Java: JUnit 5 and Selenium. I will analyze your specific requirements and provide tailored recommendations.

faq.question: How do I convince my team to adopt TDD?
faq.answer: Start with pilot projects demonstrating measurable benefits: reduced bug rates, faster refactoring confidence, and living documentation. Track TDD metrics like cycle time and test coverage. Provide training through TDD katas and pair programming sessions.

faq.question: Are AI-powered testing tools worth the investment?
faq.answer: AI testing tools excel at reducing maintenance overhead through self-healing selectors and automated test generation. They work best for UI-heavy applications with frequent changes. Evaluate ROI based on your test flakiness rate and maintenance time.

faq.question: How can I reduce flaky tests in CI?
faq.answer: Address root causes: add explicit waits instead of fixed delays, ensure test isolation with proper cleanup, mock external dependencies, and implement retry mechanisms for known transient failures. Monitor flakiness metrics to track improvement.

faq.question: What is the optimal test coverage percentage?
faq.answer: Coverage percentage alone is misleading. Focus on testing critical paths, edge cases, and business logic. Aim for 80% on core modules while ensuring 100% coverage on high-risk areas like payment processing and authentication.

faq.question: How do I test microservices effectively?
faq.answer: Use contract testing with Pact for service communication, implement consumer-driven contracts, test services in isolation with test containers, and validate full integration with periodic E2E tests. Prioritize API contract stability.
